whatThisIs: Translate the jargon on an Explanation of Benefits document into plain English. Sophie Well covers the terms a patient or new billing-office staffer routinely encounters - billed charge, allowed amount, plan discount, deductible applied, coinsurance, copay, out-of-pocket maximum, patient responsibility, network status, claim-adjustment reason codes (CARC), and remittance advice remark codes (RARC) - with one-line definitions and the relationship between them so the math on the EOB stops looking arbitrary.
whenToUse: Use this when a patient is reading their EOB and wants to know why "allowed amount" is lower than "billed charge" but they still owe money, when a new billing-office hire is trying to learn the vocabulary fast, or when a navigator is explaining to a patient that the EOB is not a bill (a near-universal point of confusion). The EOB is the document that turns an insurance plan from a marketing brochure into a number a patient owes - the glossary is the bare-minimum vocabulary to read one accurately.
